steps.text: Find the median position. With 33 fish ordered by weight, the median is at position \(\frac{33+1}{2} = 17\). The 17th lightest fish determines the median.

Map positions to tanks: Tank A has 10 fish (positions 1–10, weight \(< 5\) oz); Tank B has 11 fish (positions 11–21, weight 5–13 oz); Tank C has 12 fish (positions 22–33, weight \(> 13\) oz). Position 17 falls in Tank B, so the median must be between 5 and 13 oz inclusive.

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: 4.5 oz — below 5 oz; this is in Tank A's weight range, not Tank B. The 17th fish cannot weigh 4.5 oz. Eliminated.
  • B: 8 oz — between 5 and 13 oz; this is a valid Tank B weight for the 17th fish. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C: 13.5 oz — above 13 oz; this falls in Tank C's weight range. The 17th fish cannot weigh 13.5 oz. Eliminated.
  • D: 15 oz — above 13 oz; also in Tank C's range. Eliminated.
